metadata_information.title: On the Welfare Costs of Premature Deindustrialization
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: On the Welfare Costs of Premature Deindustrialization

document_description.abstract: Developing countries are deindustrializing at earlier stages of development than experienced by advanced economies. Is this trend symptomatic of inefficiency? If so, what are the welfare costs? This paper proposes a definition of premature deindustrialization based on whether the pace of deindustrialization diverges from the one implied by a theoretical benchmark of efficient sectoral allocation. It identifies 10 episodes of premature deindustrialization, carrying negligible welfare costs, below 1 percent of aggregate consumption.
